pipeline.verificationNotes:
Acceptance criteria check FAILED — 58/58 AC incomplete:

### Tier table config section + validation (generalizes complexity_routing) + supervisor policy, off by default (4/4 incomplete)
  - [ ] Given a config where a difficulty maps to zero or to two tiers, loading rejects it with a named validation error identifying the offending difficulty
  - [ ] Given a tier with an unknown model or harness, loading rejects it, and a pi+Anthropic+subscription tier is rejected by the existing ToS gate
  - [ ] With no tiered_execution block present, the loader returns enabled=false and defaults to replay_threshold=0.5 without error
  - [ ] For a valid config, the validator returns a map from each of the five difficulties to its tier name plus the supervisor policy

### Add typed metadata.kind subject-matter field + planner emits it (3/3 incomplete)
  - [ ] VBriefItemMetadata.kind is typed to the nine-value enum and typecheck passes for an item that sets kind:'docs'
  - [ ] An item with no kind still validates (field optional) and downstream code defaults to 'backend'
  - [ ] The planning prompt renders an instruction for the planner to emit kind per item and defaults to 'backend' when unspecified

### Model+harness resolution chain: override -> byKind -> byDifficulty -> role default (fail-loud, no hardcoded fallback) (4/4 incomplete)
  - [ ] Given an item with metadata.model set, resolveTier returns that model regardless of kind/difficulty (the override wins)
  - [ ] Given no override but a byKind entry for the item's kind, resolveTier returns the byKind model and it wins over the byDifficulty model
  - [ ] Given only difficulty, resolveTier returns the tier whose difficulties[] contains it, with that tier's model and harness
  - [ ] Given an item that resolves to nothing and no configured default, resolveTier throws a named error and returns no model literal

### Route the hardcoded complexityToModel / claude-only path through the new chain, preserving disabled-mode behavior (3/3 incomplete)
  - [ ] With tiering disabled, resolving each of the five difficulties returns exactly today's model (trivial/simple->haiku, medium/complex->sonnet, expert->opus)
  - [ ] router.ts resolves its model through resolveTier and no longer contains the inline complexityToModel switch
  - [ ] With tiering disabled, resolveTier returns the pre-change model for each difficulty and preserves the prior mapping

### Wire resolved tier (model+harness) into slot dispatch so difficulty actually selects the worker (3/3 incomplete)
  - [ ] When tiered_execution is enabled, resolving a bead of difficulty=expert returns the expert tier's model+harness in the spawn params
  - [ ] When tiered_execution is disabled, chooseDispatchTier returns exactly its current 'in-context'|'registered-slot' result with no model override
  - [ ] A dispatched bead's spawn params carry the resolved model and harness (not the parent default) when tiering is on

### Standing tier agents: spawn+track one long-lived registered slot per tier in the plan (lazy) (4/4 incomplete)
  - [ ] Given a plan schedule, the manager returns exactly the set of tier names it contains and spawns no tier absent from the schedule
  - [ ] A tier's standing session spawns lazily (when its first run is within one run) and is reused for subsequent beads of that tier rather than respawned
  - [ ] dispatchBeadToTier routes a bead to the registered slot for its tier and returns that slot's agent id
  - [ ] Each standing tier slot spawns as a recoverable registered agent (state file + tmux session) and the dashboard shows it under the issue

### Commit feed: broadcast each commit diff to all standing tiers, ingestion-only (3/3 incomplete)
  - [ ] broadcastCommit sends the commit's diff to every standing tier agent via deliverAgentMessage (verified on a delivery spy)
  - [ ] Each feed message marks itself ingestion-only (instructs the recipient not to respond) and produces no response turn
  - [ ] Given N standing tiers and one commit, broadcastCommit sends exactly N deliveries (everyone hears everything, no filtering)

### Foreman protocol: dispatch each bead to its tier's standing agent and broadcast the resulting commit (3/3 incomplete)
  - [ ] roles/work.md contains the tiered dispatch loop (resolve tier -> dispatch to standing agent -> foreman commits -> broadcast feed), gated on tiered_execution.enabled
  - [ ] Each dispatched bead produces exactly one foreman-authored commit, preserving the one-bead-one-commit invariant
  - [ ] With tiered_execution disabled, roles/work.md preserves its existing serial and swarm paths and the default flow contains no edits

### Standing supervisor agent: spawn + subscription policy (all | flagged | sampled) (3/3 incomplete)
  - [ ] Under policy 'flagged', shouldSupervise returns true only for beads with requiresInspection=true
  - [ ] Under policy 'all', shouldSupervise returns true for every bead
  - [ ] The supervisor spawns as a registered, dashboard-discoverable agent using the tier-table supervisor model+harness

### Supervisor reviews commit vs bead ACs and posts ack / blocking finding to the inspect-status surface (3/3 incomplete)
  - [ ] A passing review persists an ack via the existing inspectStatus/onInspectComplete surface and saves the checkpoint (no new endpoint)
  - [ ] A failing review records a blocking finding on the inspect-status surface and does not change tracker (Linear/GitHub) status
  - [ ] For each subscribed commit, the supervisor receives the bead's acceptance criteria alongside the diff

### A blocking supervisor finding halts dispatch of dependent beads until a fix commit lands (3/3 incomplete)
  - [ ] Given an unresolved blocking finding on bead X and a next bead that depends on X, shouldHaltDispatch returns true
  - [ ] Once a fix commit for X lands and is acked, shouldHaltDispatch returns false and dependent dispatch resumes
  - [ ] A blocking finding on a bead with no dependents permits unrelated dispatch (shouldHaltDispatch returns false)

### Trivial fast-track: batch consecutive trivial mechanical beads into one dispatch (PAN-1311) (3/3 incomplete)
  - [ ] groupFastTrack produces a batch of consecutive trivial, scope-disjoint beads that share one fastTrackBatchKey
  - [ ] groupFastTrack rejects a bead whose files_scope overlaps another in the candidate batch (no intra-batch conflicts)
  - [ ] groupFastTrack rejects any non-trivial (medium or harder) bead from a fast-track batch

### Fail-open: a fast-track item that turns out non-trivial escalates to the standard tier (2/2 incomplete)
  - [ ] An item flagged non-trivial mid-flight exits its fast-track batch and routes to the standard tier
  - [ ] An escalated item routes through full review and the auto-merge path refuses it

### Trivial-tier typecheck-gated auto-merge, skip convoy (config-gated, off by default) (4/4 incomplete)
  - [ ] With tiered_execution disabled, the auto-merge path refuses all items and every item routes through the normal review-then-merge flow
  - [ ] A trivial batch that passes typecheck and lint auto-merges to the feature branch without the review convoy
  - [ ] A trivial batch that fails the typecheck gate is not merged and surfaces back to the foreman
  - [ ] The auto-merge path rejects any batch that contains a medium-or-harder bead

### Replay: respawn dead standing agent + re-deliver commit feed; threshold-triggered compaction at run boundaries (3/3 incomplete)
  - [ ] When a standing tier agent is killed mid-issue, replay spawns a dispatch-ready session and re-sends the feed equal to git log base..HEAD in order
  - [ ] Replay triggers at a tier-run boundary when estimated context exceeds replay_threshold, and never triggers mid-bead
  - [ ] The supervisor replay spawns it through the same path and re-sends the subscription-filtered commit history

### Measurement scaffolding: stamp feed deliveries + per-tier cost rows (warm-hit fraction) (3/3 incomplete)
  - [ ] Each feed delivery records a timestamp and token count queryable per standing agent
  - [ ] The system produces a per-agent warm-hit fraction (deliveries with gap < 300s) from the delivery log
  - [ ] pan token-spend-report shows distinct per-role rows for the foreman, each tier agent, and the supervisor

### Enablement gate: with tiered_execution disabled, zero behavior change (parity lock test) (2/2 incomplete)
  - [ ] With tiering disabled, resolveTier returns the legacy difficulty->model output for all five difficulties (locked by the test)
  - [ ] When disabled, the test verifies no standing-tier or supervisor spawns and no auto-merge branch executes

### Document tiered execution (config, tiers, supervisor, fast-track, replay) in the docs site (2/2 incomplete)
  - [ ] The doc renders a fenced config example that contains the tiered_execution keys enabled, tiers, supervisor, and replay_threshold
  - [ ] The doc renders a Non-goals section that names per-tier commit filtering, the live cost pilot, and remote tiers as deferred

### End-to-end: a fixture plan runs through tiered execution (warm tiers + supervisor + one fast-track batch) (3/3 incomplete)
  - [ ] The e2e verifies each fixture bead routes to the standing agent of the tier its difficulty resolves
  - [ ] The e2e verifies every commit is broadcast to all standing tiers and the supervisor emits a verdict per subscribed commit
  - [ ] The e2e verifies a blocking supervisor finding blocks dependent dispatch until a fix commit, and a killed standing agent respawns to dispatch-ready
